issue.title: Pipeline Timeline: replace step numbers with step-specific icons (E55)

phases.title: PipelineTimeline: inline SVG icons replace 1-6 digits + add step-name tooltip
phases.content:
Replace the `index + 1` digit at `PipelineTimeline.tsx:159` with a step-specific inline SVG, keep `\u2713` for the completed state (Option A from the review — lower risk and explicitly endorsed by the issue), and add `title={step}` to the circle `
` to close the pre-existing tooltip gap. ### `STEP_ICONS` map (add near `STEP_LABELS`) A `Record` mapping each step to a single self-contained `
{state === "completed" ? "\u2713" : index + 1}
``` New (also adds the missing `title` attribute — a pre-existing accessibility/UX gap per review #5): ```tsx
{state === "completed" ? "\u2713" : STEP_ICONS[step]}
``` Use `STEP_LABELS[step]` (the existing display label like "Get", "Review", etc.) for the tooltip, not the raw `step` key — friendlier hover text. ### Completed-state handling: Option A (preserve checkmark) The `.completing` animation (`step-complete` keyframe) is named "checkmark pop" in the CSS — keep `\u2713` for completed. Step icons appear only for `pending`, `active`, and `failed` states. This is what the issue's acceptance-criteria explicitly says to do ("Completed steps show ✓ (existing behavior preserved) OR the step glyph in a 'completed' tint — pick one and stay consistent"). Picking ✓. ### Color inheritance All six SVGs use `stroke="currentColor"` (and `fill="currentColor"` for the play triangle, with overrides set on EXECUTE) — they automatically inherit the existing E49 status-color classes (`.pipeline-step-circle.pending`, `.active`, `.completed`, `.failed`) without any new CSS. Both light and dark themes work via existing `index.css` overrides. ### Scope boundaries - **One file:** `apps/desktop/src/components/detail/PipelineTimeline.tsx`. No CSS changes expected — the parent circle already has `display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center`, so the SVG centers itself. - No animation changes — `.step-activate`, `.step-complete`, `.pipeline-pulse`, `.connector-fill` operate on the circle container, not its children; the icon scales with the circle automatically. - No new dependencies — every SVG path is inlined, matching the project's existing convention (zero icon-library imports in the codebase per the review). - `prefers-reduced-motion` already covered by the global `index.css` rule; no new motion surface introduced. ### Test plan - `pnpm build` — clean tsc -b + vite. Watch for TS6133. - `pnpm test` — 89 desktop vitest baseline preserved. No new snapshot tests required (consistent with how E2/E3/E10/E11/E14 shipped in v0.6.0 without per-component snapshots). - Manual smoke: (a) open an issue with active work → see the play triangle inside the active circle, with pulse animation; (b) advance past EXECUTE → completed step shows checkmark (existing pop animation); (c) hover any circle → tooltip shows step label (Get/Review/Plan/Audit/Execute/Ship); (d) toggle light theme → all six glyphs remain legible against their colored backgrounds; (e) check that the play triangle (EXECUTE) is visually distinct from the clipboard-check (AUDIT) and the paper-plane (SHIP) at 14 px rendered. ### Final issue of v0.6.1 This is the last issue of the v0.6.1 cascade. After SHIP completes, the release-ship logic kicks in: archive the release file, tag v0.6.1, generate changelog, ship to GitHub Releases.
verification: `PipelineTimeline.tsx`: a `STEP_ICONS: Record<PipelineStep, React.ReactNode>` constant exists near `STEP_LABELS`, with six entries (GET, REVIEW, PLAN, AUDIT, EXECUTE, SHIP), each rendering an inline `<svg>` using `viewBox="0 0 24 24"`, `stroke="currentColor"`, `strokeWidth="2"`.
Each SVG renders at `width="14" height="14"` and uses `currentColor` exclusively — no hardcoded hex.
Render expression at line ~159 becomes `state === "completed" ? "\u2713" : STEP_ICONS[step]` — checkmark preserved for completed state.
The circle `<div>` gains a `title={STEP_LABELS[step]}` attribute (closes the pre-existing tooltip gap the issue's hover criterion calls out).
Status colors continue to drive the icon stroke (no CSS changes required); animations (`step-activate`, `step-complete`, `pipeline-pulse`, `connector-fill`) unchanged.
No new files, no new dependencies, no CSS changes.
`pnpm build` passes (tsc -b strict + vite); `pnpm test` is green (89 desktop tests preserved).

Manual smoke verified: each step's icon is recognizable at 14 px, the checkmark + pop animation still works for completed, the tooltip shows the step's display label on hover, and both themes render legibly.
phases.summary: Added `STEP_ICONS: Record<PipelineStep, React.ReactNode>` map with six inline Heroicons-style SVGs (cloud-arrow-down, magnifying-glass, bullet-list, clipboard-check, play-triangle, paper-airplane) plus a shared `ICON_SVG_PROPS` const in `PipelineTimeline.tsx`; swapped the `index + 1` digit at line ~159 for `STEP_ICONS[step]` (checkmark preserved for completed); and added `title={STEP_LABELS[step]}` to the `.pipeline-step-circle` to close the pre-existing tooltip gap. `pnpm build` (tsc -b + vite) clean — no TS warnings. `pnpm test` green: shared 65/65, desktop vitest 89/89 (baseline preserved), Rust 21+7 tests all passing.
